---
title: "10 Percent BSA Protocol"
author: "Kevin Shih"
date: "`r format(Sys.time(), '%d %B %Y')`"
output: 
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 5
    toc_float: true
    number_sections: false
    df_print: paged
    code_folding: hide
    highlight: pygments
 
---

# Materials

-	Powdered and purified BSA
-	PBS (can use RNAase-free PBS) or water
-	Weigh Boat
-	Scale




# Steps

1. Determine total volume to make  
   a. To make 50 mL of 10% BSA in PBS will require 5 grams of BSA
  
2. Put about 80% of the base (water or PBS) in a tube
3. Using a weigh boat, weigh out the amount of BSA needed
4. Add small amounts of the BSA into the tube and let fully dissolve before adding more to prevent clumping or bubbles
5. Adjust the volume to the final volume desired
6. Store in 4ºC – may be frozen in smaller aliquots



This was last edited on 7/10/2026 by Kevin Shih
